question_title: How much will Nvidia's stock price returns exceed Apple's in these biweekly periods of Q1 2026? (Jan 19 - Jan 30)
summary: Forecasting the difference in biweekly returns (NVDA - AAPL) for Jan 19-30, 2026. Based on recent daily return data: NVDA has ~1.92% daily volatility vs AAPL's ~0.84%, with low correlation (~0.19), yielding a spread standard deviation of ~1.94%/day or ~6.15pp over 10 trading days. Adjusting upward for fat tails and Apple earnings likely occurring near the end of the period (Jan 29-30), I use std ~7pp. The mean is centered slightly positive (~0.5pp) reflecting NVDA's AI/growth momentum and CES 2026 afterglow, though this is essentially noise given the uncertainty. Apple earnings add symmetric tail risk to AAPL specifically. The distribution is approximately normal, wide, and centered near zero.

factors.description: NVDA has ~2.3x higher daily volatility than AAPL (1.92% vs 0.84%), driving wide spread distribution with ~6.15pp 10-day std
Low NVDA-AAPL correlation (~0.19) means spread variance is nearly the sum of individual variances, keeping uncertainty high
NVDA has outperformed AAPL over past year (33% vs 10%), suggesting slight structural growth advantage favoring positive resolution
CES 2026 (typically Jan 6-9) precedes this period; NVDA usually has major AI keynote that could boost momentum into Jan 19-30
Apple likely reports Q1 FY2026 earnings around Jan 29-30, right at end of period - adds ~3-5% potential AAPL move in either direction, increasing spread uncertainty
Recent 30-day data shows NVDA and AAPL returning similar amounts (+1.25% vs +0.91%), with daily excess return of 0.026% being statistically indistinguishable from zero
